meta.title: Will Eating Less Make You Live Longer
meta.sourceDoc: 120. P2 - Will Eating Less Make You Live Longer 节食与长寿.pdf
meta.matchedTitle: Will Eating Less Make You Live Longer 节食与长寿

passageNotes.label: Source
passageNotes.text: Explanations were extracted from the answer-analysis table in the source PDF by OCR. Minor OCR mistakes may remain; the official answer key is used for the answer field.

questionSections.text:
题目 14
答案：B
PDF 定位："In mice many experiments had come to the same finding: feed them a diet with 30% fewer calories and see a lifespan extension of 40%. The monkey trials were set up in a similar way:... cut it by 30% .."(第B段)
PDF 解析：题干要"相同幅度的热量减少在不同动物上的研究"。B段先说鼠的实验统一减 少30%，再说猴的试验以相同方式设置（同样30%)，完全对应。

题目 15
答案：G
PDF 定位："several of the NIA control monkeys have lived past the age of 4o, far exceeding the 27-year average lifespan for captive rhesus monkeys." (第G段)
PDF 解析：题干要"对照组某些猴子高于同类正常寿命的评论"。G段明确：对照猴活过 40岁，远超27年的平均寿命。

题目 16
答案：E
PDF 定位："Second, whereas the Wisconsin monkeys were given highly processed food high in sucrose, .. the NIA diet was based on whole grains, fish oils, and was very low in sugar. These different settings .. may provide an explanation of why the two groups showed different results."（第E段）
PDF 解析：题干要"食物类型差异并可能导致结果对比"。E段逐点对比两研究的食物组成 （高蔗糖加工食品VS.全谷物/鱼油/低糖）并明确说这或可解释结果差异。

题目 17
答案：C
PDF 定位：.. the lean monkeys were living longer and suffering less from the diseases of ageing: diabetes, heart disease and brain diseases." (第C段)
PDF 解析：题干要"热量限制猴子更不易得到的健康问题例子"。C段给出具体疾病：糖尿 病、心脏病、脑部疾病。

题目 18
答案：A
PDF 定位："Rozalyn Anderson ... says the research is not intended as a recommendation of calorie restriction.'I find the idea monumentally unattractive!"（第A段）
PDF 解析：题干要"研究者对热量限制的负面看法"。A段安德森直言"我觉得这个想法极 不吸引人"，即负面态度。

题目 19
答案：D
PDF 定位："The Wisconsin study began with monkeys in early adulthood." (第D段)
PDF 解析：题干要"研究开始时猴子所处生命阶段"。D段直接给出：在成年早期开始。

questionSections.text:
题目 20
答案：A
PDF 定位："The fact the two studies were set up differently, asking the same question in different ways I think we will gain maximally from that."(第1段)
PDF 解析：她认为两项研究"做法不同"是好事，能带来最大化收益，符合 "dissimilar approaches is good"。

题目 21
答案：C
PDF 定位："However, neither group had the right notion of a standard diet ... the NIA controls were ... leaner than a control monkey should be... the Wisconsin controls were a little bigger than they had to be."（第H段）
PDF 解析：她指出两组都没用"标准"饮食（即不典型），对应"Neitherstudy used diets that are typical。

题目 22
答案：A
PDF 定位："We study it because it is so effective at delaying ageing and the onset of age-related disease. It's a way to tease out what ... creates increased disease vulnerability as a function of age."(第A段）
PDF 解析：她把热量限制当作"揭示年龄相关疾病机制的工具"，符合"a method of finding out about health issues connected with ageing"。

题目 23
答案：B
PDF 定位：" Julie Mattison ...observes that an overweight person who goes to a fast food restaurant every day will obviously benefit if their calories are cut back by30%."(第F段）
PDF 解析：她明确说对不健康饮食/超重的人，减少热量会有积极效果，完 全对应题干。

questionSections.text:
题目 24
答案：ageing
PDF 定位："in both cases the calorie-restricted monkeys had fewer diseasesrelatedtoageing."（第I段；亦可参见第C段 "diseases of ageing")
PDF 解析：题干"与....过程相关的疾病"即"ageing（衰老）过程"。

题目 25
答案：lifespan
PDF 定位："As to the question of whether calorie restriction extends lifespan in primates, Anderson and Mattison are ... working together..." (第1段)
PDF 解析：摘要句式为"increaseprimates"，与原文"extend lifespan"同义替换；词源自原文。

题目 26
答案：conflict
PDF 定位："The current data conflict will ultimately provide deeper insights into ageing .." (第1段)
PDF 解析：摘要"Although there is a, between the two sets of data"="dataconflict"。用文中原词conflict最稳妥。
